exampleEnglish: As a citizen I have rights.
memoryTip: 'Bürger' from 'Burg' (fortress/town) — one who lives in the town.
grammarNote: der Bürger; plural: die Bürger (f: die Bürgerin)

memoryTip: Already at 510 as personal energy; this usage is for physical/power energy.

usageNote: 'die Konsequenzen tragen' = to face the consequences.
'unter dem Einfluss von' = under the influence of.
'Marktanteil' = market share; 'Anteil haben an' = to have a share in.
'Verlustgeschäft' = loss-making business; 'Verluste erleiden' = to suffer losses.
'Lebenshaltungskosten' = cost of living; 'Kosten decken' = to cover costs.
'im Budgetrahmen bleiben' = to stay within budget.

memoryTip: 'Steuer' also means 'steering wheel' — the state steers via taxes.

exampleEnglish: I am writing a job application.
The job interview is going well.
usageNote: 'zum Vorstellungsgespräch eingeladen werden' = to be invited for interview.

exampleEnglish: She is having a good career.
usageNote: 'Karriereleiter' = career ladder; 'Karriere machen' = to climb the career ladder.
'Studium abschließen' = to complete a degree; 'Hochschule' = university/college.

english: the conclusion, the qualification, the degree

usageNote: 'Prüfung bestehen' = to pass an exam; 'Abschlussprüfung' = final exam.

memoryTip: 'Fach' = compartment/subject — like a pigeon-hole for each subject.

exampleGerman: Ich bin in der fünften Klasse.
exampleEnglish: I am in the fifth grade.
I am writing a term paper.
She does research at the university.
We are experimenting with new methods.

usageNote: 'Schätzung' = estimate; 'ich schätze, dass...' = I reckon that...

english: to promote, to support, to fund

usageNote: 'Umsetzung' = implementation; 'Ideen umsetzen' = to put ideas into practice.

exampleEnglish: We are investing in the future.

english: to lead, to manage, to conduct

usageNote: 'Evaluation' = evaluation; 'evaluieren und verbessern' = to evaluate and improve.

exampleGerman: Ich konzentriere mich auf die Arbeit.

exampleEnglish: How do you react to that?

memoryTip: 'Zweifeln' from 'zwei' (two) — split in two opinions = doubt.

usageNote: 'Ablehnung' = rejection; 'einen Antrag ablehnen' = to reject an application.

exampleEnglish: I am applying for a visa.

usageNote: 'Ich finde heraus, warum...' = I will find out why...
'Rückfrage' = follow-up question; 'beim Arzt nachfragen' = to ask the doctor.
'eine Hypothese aufstellen' = to formulate a hypothesis.
'Antithese' = antithesis; 'These und Antithese' = thesis and antithesis.

grammarNote: die Praxis; plural: die Praxen (offices) / Praxises
usageNote: 'in der Praxis' = in practice; 'Arztpraxis' = doctor's surgery.
'transparent' = transparent; 'für Transparenz sorgen' = to ensure transparency.

exampleEnglish: The infrastructure needs to be improved.

usageNote: 'Potenzial ausschöpfen' = to tap potential; 'Potenzialanalyse' = potential analysis.

exampleEnglish: The capacity is at full stretch.

usageNote: 'einen Kompromiss schließen' = to reach a compromise.

memoryTip: 'Scheitern' from 'Scheit' (log) — a ship running aground on logs.
usageNote: 'gescheitert' = failed; 'an etwas scheitern' = to fail at something.

exampleGerman: Ich bin kritisch gegenüber dieser Idee.
exampleEnglish: I am critical of this idea.

usageNote: 'nachvollziehen' = to understand/follow along; 'Schritt für Schritt nachvollziehen' = to trace step by step.

memoryTip: 'Ausschlag' (deflection/swing) + 'geben' = the factor that tips the scales.
'Vorläufig' = provisionally running ahead — not yet final.
'End-gültig' = finally valid — valid at the end.

exampleGerman: Das ist nur eine kurzfristige Lösung.
exampleEnglish: That is only a short-term solution.
usageNote: 'auf kurzfristige Sicht' = in the short term.

exampleGerman: Das ist Teil des öffentlichen Diskurses.
exampleEnglish: That is part of public discourse.

english: the standpoint, the point of view
exampleEnglish: That is my point of view.
memoryTip: 'Stand' (stand) + 'Punkt' (point) = the point from which you stand.
usageNote: 'seinen Standpunkt vertreten' = to advocate one's viewpoint.
'auf der Grundlage von' = on the basis of; 'Grundlagenforschung' = basic research.

exampleGerman: Das ist das Wesen der Sache.
exampleEnglish: That is the essence of the matter.

usageNote: 'im Trend liegen' = to be on trend; 'Trendwende' = turning point.
'normieren' = to standardise; 'Normabweichung' = deviation from the norm.

exampleEnglish: A paradigm shift is taking place.

usageNote: 'metaphorisch' = metaphorical; 'eine Metapher verwenden' = to use a metaphor.
'kontextabhängig' = context-dependent; 'im richtigen Kontext' = in the right context.

memoryTip: 'Fazit' = (sum total) from Latin — the bottom line.
usageNote: 'ein Fazit ziehen' = to draw a conclusion; 'als Fazit lässt sich sagen' = to conclude...
'eine Einschätzung abgeben' = to give an assessment.

exampleGerman: Bitte geben Sie eine Stellungnahme ab.
usageNote: 'eine Stellungnahme verfassen' = to write a statement.

exampleEnglish: I am weighing up the options.
usageNote: 'Abwägung' = weighing up; 'Pro und Kontra abwägen' = to weigh pros and cons.

english: to put into perspective, to relativise
exampleEnglish: You have to put that into perspective.

usageNote: 'Berücksichtigung' = consideration; 'unter Berücksichtigung von' = taking into account.
'Einbeziehung' = inclusion; 'alle Beteiligten einbeziehen' = to involve all stakeholders.

memoryTip: 'Auf' (up) + 'wand' (wall) — the wall you build up = effort.
Already defined — reinforced here in analysis context.

usageNote: 'konstruktive Rückmeldung' = constructive feedback; 'Feedback geben' = to give feedback.
'unter der Annahme, dass...' = on the assumption that...
'von einer Prämisse ausgehen' = to start from a premise.

english: the state of affairs, the facts
usageNote: 'den Sachverhalt klären' = to clarify the facts.

exampleGerman: Unter diesen Umständen ist das schwer.
exampleEnglish: Under these circumstances that is difficult.
usageNote: 'unter Umständen' = possibly/under certain circumstances; 'Umstände machen' = to cause inconvenience.
'Vereinbarung treffen' = to make an agreement; 'Geheimvereinbarung' = secret agreement.

memoryTip: 'Vertrag' from 'vertragen' (tolerate/bear) — what both sides can bear.
usageNote: 'Arbeitsvertrag' = employment contract; 'Vertrag kündigen' = to terminate a contract.
'Gutachter' = expert assessor; 'ein Gutachten erstellen' = to prepare an expert report.

english: the constitution, the state, the condition
exampleEnglish: The Basic Law is our constitution.
usageNote: 'Verfassungsgericht' = constitutional court; 'in guter Verfassung sein' = to be in good shape.

memoryTip: 'Satzung' from 'setzen' (set) — what has been set down/enacted.
usageNote: 'Vereinssatzung' = club charter; 'laut Satzung' = according to the statutes.
'Sicherheitsvorschriften' = safety regulations; 'Vorschriften einhalten' = to comply with regulations.
'EU-Richtlinie' = EU directive; 'Richtlinien festlegen' = to set guidelines.
'Bundesbehörde' = federal authority; 'Behördengang' = trip to a government office.

exampleGerman: Das geht an die nächste Instanz.
exampleEnglish: That goes to the next authority.
usageNote: 'erste Instanz' = court of first instance; 'Instanzenweg' = chain of command.

memoryTip: 'Berufung' from 'berufen' (call/appoint) — the calling to a higher court.
usageNote: 'Berufungsgericht' = court of appeal; 'eine Berufung haben' = to have a vocation.

memoryTip: 'Bescheid' from 'bescheiden' — what is communicated as a decision.
usageNote: 'Steuerbescheid' = tax assessment; 'Bescheid geben' = to let someone know.

english: the goodwill, the gesture of goodwill
exampleEnglish: That will be refunded as a goodwill gesture.
memoryTip: 'Kulanz' = goodwill (business term) — going beyond strict obligation.
usageNote: 'aus Kulanz' = as a goodwill gesture; 'Kulanzleistung' = goodwill payment.
'Protokoll führen' = to keep minutes; 'laut Protokoll' = according to the minutes.
'Jahresbericht' = annual report; 'Bericht erstatten' = to make a report.
'Ausweisdokument' = identity document; 'Dokumente archivieren' = to archive documents.

english: the attachment, the facility, the investment
usageNote: 'als Anlage beifügen' = to attach; 'Kapitalanlage' = capital investment.

memoryTip: 'Entwurf' from 'entwerfen' (design/draft) — what is thrown out first.

usageNote: 'eine klare Formulierung finden' = to find a clear formulation.
'im genauen Wortlaut' = in the exact wording.

exampleEnglish: In the first section it says...
usageNote: 'Gesetzesabschnitt' = section of law; 'Lebensabschnitt' = phase of life.
'Verweis' = reference/reprimand; 'auf jemanden verweisen' = to refer to someone.

exampleEnglish: I would like to note that...

usageNote: 'Erläuterung' = explanation; 'ausführlich erläutern' = to elaborate in detail.

english: to execute, to carry out, to explain in detail

usageNote: 'Betonung' = emphasis/stress; 'mit Nachdruck betonen' = to strongly emphasise.
'Hervorhebung' = highlighting; 'fett hervorheben' = to highlight in bold.
'Unterstreichung' = underlining; 'einen Punkt unterstreichen' = to underscore a point.

english: to refer to, to relate to
usageNote: 'Bezug nehmen auf' = to refer to; 'in Bezug auf' = with regard to.

exampleEnglish: As a result we need to replan.
memoryTip: 'In Folge dessen' = in the wake of that = consequently.

exampleGerman: Das erste Argument ist stark, hingegen...
exampleEnglish: The first argument is strong; on the other hand...
usageNote: Used to introduce a contrasting view.

memoryTip: 'Nichts desto trotz' = nothing the less defiant — a great compound!

usageNote: 'allerdings' can mean 'admittedly' or 'however' depending on position.

exampleEnglish: That would have been the case anyway.
memoryTip: 'Ohne' (without) + 'hin' = going there without any reason = anyway.

exampleEnglish: That is by now well known.
In the meantime much has changed.
First of all we must analyse.

usageNote: 'die bisherige Methode' = the method used until now.

exampleEnglish: We are working on it in parallel.
We are proceeding step by step.
Due to the weather we are staying.
memoryTip: 'Auf' (on) + 'Grund' (ground/reason) = on the basis of = due to.
usageNote: 'aufgrund der Tatsache' = due to the fact.

exampleEnglish: As a result of the accident...
memoryTip: 'In Folge' (in consequence) = as a result of.
'An-gesichts' = in the face of = given/in view of.

usageNote: Formal register; 'in Bezug auf' = simpler alternative.
Very common in formal letters: 'Bezüglich Ihrer Anfrage vom...'

english: with a view to, in view of
usageNote: 'im Hinblick auf' = in view of/with regard to.

exampleEnglish: Within the framework of the project...

usageNote: 'im Zusammenhang stehen mit' = to be connected with.

english: in view of, with an eye to
exampleEnglish: With an eye to the future...
Against the background of the crisis...
usageNote: 'vor dem Hintergrund' = against the backdrop of.

exampleEnglish: On one hand it is good...
usageNote: Always paired with 'andererseits' = on the other hand.

exampleEnglish: ...on the other hand it is difficult.

exampleGerman: Sowohl Peter als auch Maria kommen.
exampleEnglish: Both Peter and Maria are coming.
memoryTip: 'Sowohl... als auch' = both... as well as... — inclusive.
usageNote: 'sowohl A als auch B' = both A and B.

exampleGerman: Das ist weder gut noch schlecht.
exampleEnglish: That is neither good nor bad.
usageNote: 'weder A noch B' = neither A nor B.

exampleGerman: Entweder du kommst, oder du gehst.
exampleEnglish: Either you come or you go.
usageNote: 'entweder A oder B' = either A or B.

exampleGerman: Nicht nur klug, sondern auch weise.
exampleEnglish: Not only clever, but also wise.
memoryTip: 'Nicht nur... sondern auch' = not only... but also — additive.

usageNote: 'je größer, desto besser' = the bigger, the better.

exampleEnglish: Although it is raining, I am going.

exampleGerman: Obgleich er krank war, arbeitete er.
exampleEnglish: Although he was ill, he worked.

english: whereas, while on the other hand

exampleGerman: Sobald er kommt, rufe ich an.
exampleEnglish: As soon as he comes, I will call.
usageNote: 'sobald wie möglich' = as soon as possible.

exampleEnglish: As long as you are here...

usageNote: 'bis morgen' = until tomorrow; 'bis dahin' = until then.

exampleGerman: Ich wohne hier seit drei Jahren.
exampleEnglish: I have been living here for three years.
memoryTip: 'Seit' = since — use with present tense for ongoing state.
usageNote: 'seit' + present = ongoing from past; 'seit ich hier bin' = since I've been here.

english: the side effect, the side benefit
usageNote: 'Nebenwirkung' = side effect (medical); 'unerwünschte Nebeneffekte' = unwanted side effects.
'einen Aspekt berücksichtigen' = to consider an aspect.

memoryTip: Already noted — reinforced in formal context.

exampleEnglish: In the course of the year...
usageNote: 'Krankheitsverlauf' = course of illness; 'im weiteren Verlauf' = in the further course of.

english: the period of time, the timeframe
usageNote: 'im Zeitraum von... bis...' = in the period from... to.
'Zeitplan einhalten' = to keep to the schedule; 'Zeitplanung' = time planning.
'Frist' = deadline (German alternative); 'die Deadline einhalten' = to meet the deadline.
'Frist einhalten' = to meet a deadline; 'Kündigungsfrist' = notice period.

english: the appointment, the deadline, the date
the scope, the extent, the circumference
usageNote: 'im vollen Umfang' = to the full extent; 'Umfang des Projekts' = project scope.
'in vollem Ausmaß' = to its full extent.
'eine breite Bandbreite von' = a wide range of.

english: the scope, the leeway, the room for maneuver

usageNote: 'Spielraum haben' = to have leeway; 'finanzieller Spielraum' = financial headroom.

exampleEnglish: There is an urgent need for action.
usageNote: 'Handlungsbedarf besteht' = there is a need for action.

english: the approach to solving, the solution approach
exampleEnglish: What is your approach to solving this?

english: the key message, the main point
usageNote: 'die Kernaussagen zusammenfassen' = to summarise the key messages.

memoryTip: 'Schwer' (heavy) + 'Punkt' (point) = the heavy point = centre of gravity/focus.
usageNote: 'Schwerpunkt legen auf' = to focus on; 'thematischer Schwerpunkt' = thematic focus.
'günstige Rahmenbedingungen schaffen' = to create favourable framework conditions.

exampleGerman: Das ist das Kernstück des Plans.
exampleEnglish: That is the centrepiece of the plan.

memoryTip: Already noted — reinforced in B2 context.

exampleEnglish: We must accept the given circumstances.
To clarify the state of affairs.

english: to prove to be, to turn out to be
memoryTip: 'Erweisen' = to prove/show — 'sich erweisen als' = to turn out to be.
grammarNote: Reflexive: erweist sich als; erwies sich als
usageNote: 'sich als nützlich erweisen' = to prove useful.

memoryTip: 'In Frage stellen' = to place in question = challenge.
usageNote: 'grundlegend in Frage stellen' = to fundamentally question.

memoryTip: 'Über' (over) + 'einstimmen' (tune in unison) = to be in harmony.

exampleEnglish: The opinions differ from each other.

english: to correspond to, to comply with
exampleEnglish: That does not correspond to reality.
memoryTip: 'Ent-sprechen' = to speak in return to = correspond to.
grammarNote: Irregular: entspricht; entsprach, hat entsprochen (+ dative)

usageNote: 'Umfang' = scope; 'ein breites Spektrum umfassen' = to encompass a wide spectrum.
'anhand eines Beispiels veranschaulichen' = to illustrate with an example.
Formal/academic register; 'ein Beispiel anführen' is more common.
'Konkretisierung' = specification; 'die Idee konkretisieren' = to flesh out the idea.

exampleEnglish: We need to differentiate more strongly.
We are implementing the new software.

usageNote: 'Priorisierung' = prioritisation; 'nach Priorität vorgehen' = to proceed by priority.

memoryTip: Already noted — reinforced in management context.

english: to acquire, to win new customers

exampleGerman: Das Modell lässt sich gut skalieren.

exampleEnglish: In conclusion I would like to summarise.
memoryTip: 'Resümieren' = résumé — to draw out the sum.

english: the economic cycle, the economic situation

usageNote: 'in eine Rezession geraten' = to fall into recession.
'Wirtschaftswachstum messen mit dem BIP' = measuring economic growth with GDP.
'bei Marktversagen eingreifen' = to intervene in case of market failure.

memoryTip: 'Transparenz' = transparency — already noted in ethics context.
usageNote: 'transparent' = transparent; 'für Transparenz sorgen' = to ensure transparency.

english: the honorary office, the voluntary work

usageNote: 'Die Würde des Menschen ist unantastbar.' = first article of the German Basic Law.
'Rechtsstaatlichkeit' = rule of law; 'Rechtsprinzip' = principle of law.
'Grundgesetzartikel' = article of the Basic Law; abbreviated GG.
Germany's upper house representing the Bundesländer.
'Bundeshaushalt' = federal budget; 'Haushalt führen' = to run a household.

exampleEnglish: The level of debt is high.

memoryTip: Already noted — reinforced in political context.
usageNote: 'fauler Kompromiss' = bad compromise; 'tragfähiger Kompromiss' = viable compromise.

exampleEnglish: There is a conflict of interest.

usageNote: 'in einem Dilemma stecken' = to be in a dilemma.
'Machtgleichgewicht' = balance of power; 'aus dem Gleichgewicht bringen' = to upset the balance.

exampleGerman: Das ist die Ironie des Schicksals.
exampleEnglish: That is the irony of fate.

usageNote: 'authentisch' = authentic; 'authentisch auftreten' = to come across as authentic.
'zur Reflexion anregen' = to encourage reflection; 'selbstreflexiv' = self-reflective.

exampleEnglish: A change in consciousness is needed.

usageNote: 'Fremdbild' = the image others have of you.

exampleEnglish: I feel a sense of belonging.

exampleGerman: Er kommt aus einer privilegierten Familie.
exampleEnglish: He comes from a privileged family.
That has been demonstrated to be correct.
That is, as is well known, difficult.

exampleGerman: Das ist de facto die Hauptstadt.
exampleEnglish: That is de facto the capital.
memoryTip: 'De facto' = in fact (Latin) — same phrase in German.
usageNote: 'de jure' = by law (opposite); 'de facto vs. de jure'.

memoryTip: 'De jure' = by law (Latin) — same phrase in German.

exampleGerman: Das ist per se nicht schlecht.
exampleEnglish: That is not bad in itself.
memoryTip: 'Per se' = through itself (Latin) — same phrase in German.
'Vice versa' = with positions reversed (Latin) — same phrase.
'Et cetera' = and the rest (Latin) — same phrase.

exampleEnglish: To speak German or rather French.
usageNote: 'bzw.' very common in German text; 'oder vielmehr' = or rather.

exampleGerman: Das ist klar, das heißt wichtig.
exampleEnglish: That is clear, that is to say important.

exampleGerman: In der Regel ist das so.
exampleEnglish: As a rule that is the case.
memoryTip: 'In der Regel' = in the rule = as a rule.
usageNote: 'regelgemäß' = according to rule; 'im Allgemeinen' = in general.

exampleGerman: Das ist in der Tat richtig.
memoryTip: 'In der Tat' = in the deed = indeed.

english: to the point, in a nutshell
exampleGerman: Auf den Punkt gebracht: Es funktioniert.
memoryTip: 'Auf den Punkt bringen' = to bring to the point.
usageNote: 'kurz und bündig' = brief and to the point.

exampleGerman: Mit anderen Worten: Es ist falsch.
exampleEnglish: In other words: It is wrong.
memoryTip: 'Mit anderen Worten' = with other words = in other words.

english: all in all, on the whole
exampleGerman: Alles in allem war es gut.
exampleEnglish: All in all it was good.
memoryTip: 'Alles in allem' = everything in everything = all in all.

english: by and large, on the whole
exampleGerman: Im Großen und Ganzen stimmt das.
exampleEnglish: By and large that is correct.
memoryTip: 'Im Großen und Ganzen' = in the big and the whole = by and large.
'Vorbild' (role model) + '-lich' = like a role model = exemplary.
usageNote: 'Vorbild' = role model; 'als Vorbild dienen' = to serve as a role model.
